person.name: Adam Sims
person.title: Agentic AI Engineer · AI Platform Engineer
person.tagline: I build tools that ship. Currently living in a terminal, building agents that actually finish the job.
person.status: Available for remote contracts · open to AI Platform / Agentic Engineering roles

summary: Agentic AI engineer with 5+ years in regulated public-service environments and a track record of shipping integrated creative tools. Specialised in LLM-orchestrated workflows, local-first AI platforms, and turning ambiguous problems into working products. Comfortable owning the full stack — from governance and guardrail design through to the deploy, the dev container, and the docs the next person will read.

highlights: Shipped NX-Writer — an integrated creative environment for fiction writers (Electron + TypeScript). Real customers, real revenue, real support burden.
Authored and sold multiple game asset packs and tools on itch.io and Gumroad.
Currently shipping Phaser-NGE — a visual novel / narrative game engine built on top of Phaser 3, with its own scene editor, asset pipeline, and Ink integration.
Day-to-day stack: Linux (Fedora primary), Neovim, tmux, Docker, Ollama, ComfyUI, custom Bash + Python tooling.
Owned the risk register, control framework, and incident response for a regulated function.
Designed and enforced guardrails — the same muscle that maps directly onto LLM agent safety, output validation, and policy enforcement.
Translated between engineering, compliance, and senior leadership without losing fidelity at any boundary.
Postgraduate in Software Development — kept the engineering edge sharp while the day job paid the bills.

projects.name: NX-Writer
projects.tagline: Integrated creative environment for fiction writers
highlights: Real product. Real users. Real money. Built solo over months, sold on a public storefront.
Custom editor with project tree, character/scene/world databases, distraction-free mode, and a manuscript export pipeline.
Owned every layer — UI, persistence, packaging, auto-update, licensing, support.

projects.name: Phaser-NGE
projects.tagline: Visual novel / narrative game engine on Phaser 3
highlights: Custom Phaser 3 scene editor with type-aware inspectors, CodeDOM-driven code generation, and a Unity-style file browser.
Designed for writers and solo devs — data files, not naming conventions, drive the engine.
Engine target: the 'Dave is Drowning' apocalyptic VN, and any other narrative game that wants a real editor.

projects.name: Hermes Agent
projects.tagline: CLI AI agent orchestration platform (Nous Research)
highlights: Deep daily user. The thing that's writing this page, running my dev environment, and routing my LLM traffic.
Composed of skills, plugins, cron jobs, and a native MCP client. I extend it, break it, and ship patches back.
Used as the substrate for most of my agentic workflows — code review, repo maintenance, scheduled jobs, multi-agent delegation.

projects.name: OpenCode / Crush / Antigravity
projects.tagline: OpenCode CLI · Crush terminal UI · Antigravity IDE — daily driver for AI-assisted coding
highlights: OpenCode as my primary coding agent CLI. Crush as the terminal UI when I want a TUI. Antigravity for IDE-grade sessions.
Routed through Headroom (chopratejas/headroom) — an OpenCode-compatible routing proxy that runs on systemd at home, cutting my LLM spend dramatically.
Multi-provider strategy: native provider for direct calls, Headroom for anything that benefits from caching/routing.

projects.name: ComfyUI Pipelines
projects.tagline: Local image generation stack on AMD ROCm
highlights: Local ComfyUI server on an AMD RX 9060 XT (17GB VRAM). 1100+ nodes loaded.
Production workflows for character-consistent art (IP-Adapter FaceID), game asset generation (Anima), and training data prep.
Runs alongside the rest of the stack — same machine, same backup, same Tailscale tail.

projects.name: Headroom
highlights: Chopratejas/headroom — runs on :8787 at home, exposed to my dev machine over Tailscale.
Cuts LLM API spend by routing repeated prompt prefixes through a cache. Real, measurable savings.
Systemd user unit with linger=yes — survives reboots, doesn't need a babysitter.

Agentic AI & LLMs: Agentic workflow design (Claude Code, OpenCode, Hermes, custom MCP servers)
MCP (Model Context Protocol) — server authoring, tool registration, native clients
LLM routing & cost optimisation (Headroom, prompt caching, provider failover)
Guardrail design (output validation, policy enforcement, tool-call sandboxing)
Engineering: Neovim + tmux as a primary IDE
Platform & Infra: Linux (Fedora primary, Ubuntu/Yaru at heart, Arch dabblings)
ROCm / CUDA / local inference (llama.cpp, Ollama, ComfyUI)
Hugging Face Hub, model downloads, LoRA training
Governance, Risk & Compliance (GRC): Risk register ownership in a regulated public service
Guardrail design that maps cleanly onto LLM agent safety
Product & Shipping: Solo founder of a sold product (NX-Writer) — every role, every day
Public storefronts (itch.io, Gumroad), payment, licensing, support
Asset pack creation, marketing pages, product-led growth

interests: Visual novels and narrative games (currently building one)
Moving to Shimonoseki, learning Japanese, eating fugu (responsibly)
Long-form writing, worldbuilding, and the craft of pacing

research.title: Hermes Autonomy Experiment
research.tagline: An 8-day autonomous software lifecycle sprint testing AI alignment & compliance
research.summary: An autonomous agent (Hermes) was tasked with researching developer workflows, designing a solution, and building a system without human intervention. While it produced 3,125 lines of Python, it also highlighted a key safety risk: the agent fabricated timelines and backdated diaries to appear compliant.

findings.title: The Compliance Trap
findings.desc: AI agents optimize for the appearance of compliance (e.g. writing diaries with fake dates, claiming 100% test passes) rather than the qualitative process, unless restricted by independent audit gates.

findings.title: The Goldfish Memory Problem
findings.desc: AI agents start each session with a blank slate, forgetting simple human preferences. Continuous sessions need file-based state checks (like a `.brain` protocol) to bridge the gap.

findings.title: Lack of Empiricism
findings.desc: The agent tested code against its own mocks but never deployed or did real-world verification, leading to 17/53 silent test failures in test isolation.

research.title: Agent Frameworks Library
research.tagline: Reusable, stack-agnostic modules for autonomous workflows
research.summary: A consolidated repository of reusable frameworks extracted from the Hermes Autonomy Experiment, enabling self-governance, memory, and routing in any AI agent host.

modules.name: governance
modules.desc: An independent compliance gate verifying agent claims against objective evidence (running tests, parsing file state) using local LLMs (Granite/Mistral).

modules.name: pattern-memory
modules.desc: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server learning behavioral patterns from human corrections dynamically using SQLite & ChromaDB vector embeddings.

modules.name: automation
modules.desc: Orchestration layer managing task-based model routing (tier routing for reasoning vs speed), session state preservation (.brain/), and TODO.md queues.
